entries.translation: In the beginning
entries.match_notes: Cited explicitly in lecture as 'Genesis 1: In the beginning' in connection with John 1:1-5

entries.translation: And God said: 'Let there be light.' And there was light. And God saw the light, that it was good. And God separated the light from the darkness. And God called the light 'Day,' but the darkness he called 'Night.' And there was evening, and there was morning â€” one day.
entries.match_notes: Full Day 1 block rendered in distinctive 'one day' (cardinal) form; explicit Day 1 heading on slide

entries.translation: And God said: 'Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, separating between waters and waters.' And God made the expanse, and it separated between the waters which were underneath the expanse and between the waters which were above the expanse. And it was so. And God called the expanse 'Heavens.' And there was evening, and there was morning--a second day.
entries.match_notes: Verbatim block under DAY 2 heading; distinctive 'separating between waters and waters' phrasing

entries.translation: And God said: 'Let the waters under the heavens be gathered to one place, and let â€˜the dryâ€™ appear.' And it was so. And God called the dry 'Earth,' but the waters he called 'Seas.' And God saw that it was good.
entries.match_notes: Creation Act #1 block under Day 3; distinctive 'let the dry appear' phrasing

entries.translation: And God said: 'Let the earth cause sprouts to sprout: plants seeding seeds, fruit trees making fruit according to its kind whose seed is in it, upon the earth.' And it was so. And the earth brought forth sprouts: plants seeding seeds according to its kind, and trees making fruit, whose seed is in it, according to its kind.
entries.match_notes: Creation Act #2 block repeated multiple times under Day 3; distinctive 'cause sprouts to sprout' phrasing

entries.translation: And God said, 'Let there be lights in the expanse of the heavens, to separate between the day and the night; and let them be for signs, and for appointed times, and for days and years; and let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens, to give light on the earth.' And it was so.
entries.match_notes: God's Speech block repeated 5 times under Day 4 heading; distinctive 'appointed times' phrasing

entries.translation: And God made two great lights . . . the great light to be ruler of the day, and the small light to be ruler of the night, . . . and the stars. And God gave them in the expanse of the heavens, to give light on the earth, and to rule during the day and during the night, and to separate between the light and the darkness. And God saw that it was good. And there was evening, and there was morning--a fourth day.
entries.match_notes: Narrator Comments block under Day 4; distinctive 'ruler of the day/night' and 'a fourth day' phrasing

entries.translation: And God said, 'Let the waters swarm with swarming things, living creatures but flying things, let them fly over the earth upon the face of the expanse of the heavens.'
entries.match_notes: God's First Speech block under Day 5 heading; distinctive 'let the waters swarm' phrasing; repeated multiple times

entries.translation: And God created the great tannanim, and all the living creatures the creeping things which swarm the waters according to their kind, and all the flying things with wings, according to their kind, And God saw that it was good.
entries.match_notes: Narrative Comments block under Day 5; retains Hebrew 'tannanim' (sea monsters/serpents) untranslated

entries.translation: And God blessed them, saying . . . 'Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters of the seas, but the flying things, let them multiply on the earth.' And there was evening, and there was morning--a fifth day.
entries.match_notes: God's Second Speech / God's Blessing block under Day 5

entries.translation: And God said, 'Let the earth bring forth living souls according to their kind, animals, and crawling things, and living things of the earth according to their kind' and it was so. And God made the living things of the earth, according to their kind, and the animals according to their kind, and all the crawling things of the ground, according to their kind, And God saw that it was good.
entries.match_notes: Creation Act #1 block under Day 6; distinctive 'living souls' and 'crawling things' phrasing

entries.translation: And God said, 'Let us make man as our image, according to our likeness, in order that they might rule over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the heavens, and over the beasts, and over all the earth, and over all the creeping things, the creeping on the earth' And God created the man as his image, As the image of God he created him, Male and female he created them.
entries.match_notes: Creation Act #2 block under Day 6; distinctive 'as our image' (not 'in our image') phrasing; repeated

entries.translation: And God blessed them. And he said to them, 'Be fruitful, multiply, and fill the earth, and subdue it, and rule over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens, and over every living thing, those creeping upon the earth.'
entries.match_notes: God's Blessing block under Day 6; repeated multiple times

entries.translation: And God said, 'Behold, I have given to you all the herbage seeding seeds, which are on the face of all the earth, and all the trees, which in them are the fruit of trees, seeding seed. To you they will be for food. But to all the living things of the earth, and to all the birds of the heavens, and to all the creeping things upon the earth, which are in them living souls, [I have given] all the green herbs for food.' And it was so.
entries.match_notes: Food speech block under Day 6; distinctive 'herbage seeding seeds' and bracketed '[I have given]' phrasing; repeated twice

entries.translation: And God saw all which he had done, and behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning--a day, the sixth.
entries.match_notes: Concluding Statement block under Day 6; distinctive 'a day, the sixth' phrasing (article usage discussed); repeated three times

entries.translation: In the day the Lord God made the earth and the heavens, and all the shrubs of the field, before they were in the earth, and all the cultivated grains of the field, before they sprouted, (for the Lord God had not yet caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no man to serve the ground, but ground water went up from the earth and watered all the face of the earth) the Lord God formed the man, dust from the ground. And he breathed into his nostrils breath of life. And the man became a living being.
entries.match_notes: Introduction block under Gen 2 setup; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'cultivated grains of the field' and 'dust from the ground' phrasing

entries.translation: The Lord God caused to sprout from the ground all trees, pleasant to sight and good for food, and the tree of life in the middle of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and bad. Now a river goes out of Eden to water the garden. And from there it separates and becomes four heads. The name of the one, Pishon â€” It surrounds all the land of Havilah, where there is gold. (Now the gold of that land is good. Pearls and lapis lazuli are there.) And the name of the second, Gihon. It surrounds the land of Cush. And the name of the third, Tigris. It goes on the east of Assyria. And the name of the fourth, it is Euphrates.
entries.match_notes: Garden description block; distinctive 'tree of the knowledge of good and bad' and 'lapis lazuli' phrasing; ref_start corrected from 2.8 to 2.9 per verification

entries.translation: And the Lord God took the man. And he caused him to rest in the garden of Eden to serve it and to keep it.
entries.match_notes: Placement of the Man block; repeated 3 times; distinctive 'caused him to rest' phrasing

entries.translation: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying: 'From every tree of the garden you may freely eat. But from the tree of the knowledge of good and bad, you may not eat from it. For, in the day that you eat from it, you will surely die.'
entries.match_notes: Important Warning block; distinctive 'tree of the knowledge of good and bad' and 'you will surely die' phrasing

entries.translation: And the Lord God said: 'It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make him a helper that is suitable for him.' And the Lord God formed from the ground every living animal of the field, and every bird of the heavens. And he brought them to the man to see what he would call them. (Now whatever the man called each living creature, that was its name.) And the man called names to all cattle, and to the birds of the heavens, and to all the animals of the field. But, for the man, a helper suitable for him was not found.
entries.match_notes: Unexpected Complication block; repeated twice; distinctive 'helper that is suitable for him' phrasing

entries.translation: And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall on the man. And he slept. And he took one of his ribs. And he closed up the flesh in its place. And the Lord God built the rib which he took from the man into a woman. And he brought her to the man. And the man said: 'This time it is bone of my bone, and flesh of my flesh. Because of this she will be called ishah because from ish this one was taken.'
entries.match_notes: Rib/woman creation block repeated 4 times; retains Hebrew 'ishah/ish'; distinctive 'built the rib' phrasing

entries.translation: Therefore, a man will forsake his father and his mother and cling to his wife. And they will be one flesh. And the two of them, the man and his wife, were naked, and they were not ashamed.
entries.match_notes: Narrator's Comments block; repeated 3 times; distinctive 'forsake' and 'cling' phrasing

entries.translation: Now the serpent was more clever than all the animals of the field, which the Lord God had made. And he said to the woman . . .
entries.match_notes: Opening of Gen 3; distinctive 'more clever' phrasing; repeated 3 times

entries.translation: And he said to the woman: 'Did God really say, â€˜You all may not eat from every tree of the gardenâ€™?' And the woman said to the serpent: 'From the fruit of the trees of the garden we may eat. But, from the fruit of the tree that is in the middle of the garden God said, â€˜You all may not eat from it and you all may not touch it, lest you all dieâ€™' And the serpent said to the woman: 'You all will not surely die. For God knows that, in the day that you all eat from it, your eyes will be open, and you all will be like God, knowing good and bad.'
entries.match_notes: Serpent-Woman dialogue block; repeated multiple times; distinctive 'You all' plural phrasing throughout and 'you all may not touch it'

entries.translation: And the woman saw that the tree was good to eat, and that it was pleasant to the eyes and the tree was desired to make wise And she took its fruit. And she ate. And she gave to the man with her. And he ate.
entries.match_notes: Fall action block; repeated twice; distinctive 'desired to make wise' phrasing

entries.translation: And their eyes were opened. And they knew that they were naked. And they sewed together fig leaves. And they made loincloths for themselves. And they heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day. And he hid, the man and his wife, from the presence of the Lord God in the midst of the trees of the garden.
entries.match_notes: Post-fall result block; distinctive 'loincloths' and inverted word-order 'he hid, the man and his wife' preserving Hebrew emphasis

entries.translation: And the Lord God called to the man. And he said to him: 'Where are you?' And he said: 'Your voice I heard in the garden, and I saw that I was naked, and I hid.' And he said: 'Who told you that you are naked? The tree that I commanded you to not eat of it, did you eat from it?' And the man said: 'The woman that you gave to be with me, she, she gave to me from the tree. And I ate.' And the Lord God said to the woman: 'What is this you did?' And the woman said: 'The serpent deceived me. And I ate.'
entries.match_notes: Judgment dialogue block; distinctive doubled 'she, she gave' emphasizing Hebrew pronoun; Hebrew word-order preserved in 'Your voice I heard'

entries.translation: And the Lord God said to the serpent: 'Because you have done this, cursed are you above all cattle and above all the beasts of the field. Upon your belly you will go, and dirt you will eat all the days of your life. And enmity I will set between you and between the woman, and between your seed and between her seed. He will bruise your head, but you, you will bruise his heel.'
entries.match_notes: Serpent judgment block; repeated twice; distinctive 'dirt you will eat' and doubled 'you, you will bruise' phrasing

entries.translation: To the woman he said: 'I will greatly increase your toilsome labor and pregnancy. In hard labor you will bear sons, And to your husband your desire. But as for him, he will rule over you.'
entries.match_notes: Woman's judgment block; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'toilsome labor and pregnancy' combined and 'But as for him' phrasing

entries.translation: But to the man he said: 'Because . . . you have paid attention to the voice of your wife, and you ate from the tree that I commanded you saying, You will not eat from it,' cursed is the ground because of you. In toilsome labor you will eat from it all the days of your life. And thorns and thistles it will produce for you. And you will eat the herbs of the field. In the sweat of your face you will eat bread until your return to the ground, for from it you were taken. From dirt you are, and to dirt you will return.'
entries.match_notes: Man's judgment block; repeated twice; distinctive 'paid attention to the voice of your wife' and 'herbs of the field' phrasing

entries.translation: And the man called the name of his wife Havah, because she, she was the mother of all living.
entries.match_notes: Retains Hebrew 'Havah' (not Eve); doubled pronoun 'she, she was'; repeated twice with commentary

entries.translation: And the Lord God made for the man and for his wife tunics of skin, And he clothed them.
entries.match_notes: Clothing provision block; repeated twice; distinctive 'tunics of skin' phrasing

entries.translation: And the Lord God said: 'Behold, the man has become like one of us to know good and bad. And now . . . lest he send out his hand and take even from the tree of life, and eat, and live forever . . .' And the Lord God sent him from the garden eastward, to serve the ground from which he was taken. And he banished the man. And he made dwell east of the garden of Eden cherubim and a flaming whirling sword to keep the way of the tree of life.
entries.match_notes: Banishment block; repeated twice; distinctive 'flaming whirling sword' and ellipsis-interrupted divine speech preserving Hebrew suspense

entries.translation: Now the man knew Eve, his wife. And she became pregnant. And she bore Cain. And she said: 'I have created a man with the Lord.'
entries.match_notes: Explicit example in task description; repeated 3+ times; distinctive 'created a man with the Lord' (not 'gotten a man from the Lord'); Hebrew kanah = create discussed

entries.translation: And again, she bore his brother, Abel. And Abel was a caretaker of sheep, but Cain, he was a server of the ground.
entries.match_notes: Abel introduction block; repeated 3 times; distinctive 'caretaker of sheep' and 'server of the ground' phrasing; contrast emphasized

entries.translation: And it happened after some time. And Cain brought a gift to the Lord from the fruit of the ground. But Abel, he brought, even he, from the firstborn of his flock and their fat. And the Lord paid attention to Abel and his gift. But to Cain and his gift he did not pay attention.
entries.match_notes: Sacrifice block; repeated 3 times; distinctive 'paid attention' (not 'looked with favor') and 'even he' emphatic phrasing

entries.translation: And Cain was very angry. And his face fell. And the Lord said to Cain: 'Why are you angry? And why is your face fallen? Is it not that, if you do well, there will be a lifting up? But if you don't do well, sin is lying at the opening and to you its desire. But you will rule over it.'
entries.match_notes: God's warning to Cain block; repeated 5 times; distinctive 'lying at the opening' (not 'crouching at the door') and 'there will be a lifting up'

entries.translation: And Cain spoke to Abel, his brother. And it happened when they were in the field. Cain rose up against Abel, his brother. And he killed him.
entries.match_notes: Cain kills Abel narrative; distinctive 'rose up against' phrasing

entries.translation: And the Lord said to Cain: 'Where is Abel, your brother.' And he said: 'I don't know. Am I my brother's keeper?' And he said: 'What have you done?! The voice of the blood of your brother is crying to me from the ground. And now . . . Cursed are you from the ground that opened its mouth to take the blood of your brother from your hand. Though you serve the ground, it will not again give its strength to you. A roamer and wanderer you will be on the earth.'
entries.match_notes: God's second confrontation with Cain; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'voice of the blood' and 'roamer and wanderer' phrasing

entries.translation: And Cain said to the Lord: 'My punishment is too great to bear. Behold, you have banished me this day from upon the face of the ground. And from your face I will be hidden. And I will be a roamer and wanderer on the earth. And it will be that all who find me will kill me.' And the Lord said to him: 'Therefore, all who kill Cain it will be avenged seven times.' And the Lord set a sign on Cain, to not smite him, all who find him. And Cain went out from the face of the Lord. And he dwelled in a land of wandering, east of Eden.
entries.match_notes: Cain's response and exit block; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'land of wandering' (Nod interpreted) and 'smite' phrasing

entries.translation: And Cain knew his wife. And she became pregnant And she bore Enoch. And he was building a city, And he called the name of the city according to the name of his son, Enoch. And to Enoch was born Irad. And Irad begat Mekhuyael And Mekhuyael begat Methushael And Methushael begat Lamech. And Lamech took two wives, the name of one Adah, and the name of the second Zillah. And Adah bore Jabal. He was the father of the dwellers of tents and cattle. And the name of his brother, Jubal. He was the father of all who grasp harp and flute. But Zillah, even she bore Tubal Cain, sharpening all works of brass and iron. And the sister of Tubal Cain, Naamah.
entries.match_notes: Cain genealogy block; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'grasp harp and flute' (not 'play'), retains 'Mekhuyael' spelling; 'dwellers of tents and cattle'

entries.translation: And Lamech said to his wives, Adah and Zillah 'Hear my voice, wives of Lamech. Give ear to my speech. For a man I killed for my wound, and a youth for my hurt. If sevenfold will be avenged for Cain, to Lamech seventy and seven.'
entries.match_notes: Lamech's poem; distinctive 'a youth for my hurt' and 'seventy and seven' phrasing; ref_end corrected from 4.23 to 4.24 per verification

entries.translation: And Adam again knew his wife. And she bore a son. And she called his name Seth 'For God has set to me a seed in place of Abel, for Cain killed him.' Now, to Seth, even he bore a son. And he called his name Enosh. Then it began, to call on the name of the Lord.
entries.match_notes: Seth and Enosh block; repeated 5 times (also as contrast with Gen 4:1); distinctive 'God has set to me a seed' and 'Then it began, to call on the name of the Lord'

entries.translation: This is the book of the toledot of 'adam.' In the day God created 'adam,' in the likeness of God he made him. Male and female he created them. And he blessed them. And he called their name 'adam' in the day he created them.
entries.match_notes: Gen 5:1-2 block; repeated 5 times; retains Hebrew 'adam' untranslated with quotes to highlight ambiguity; explicit ref Gen 5:1 in slide heading

entries.translation: And Adam lived 130 years. And he begat in his likeness and image. And he called his name Seth. And the days of Adam after he begat Seth were 800 years. And he begat sons and daughters.
entries.match_notes: Used as an exemplar of the Gen 5 genealogy formula to illustrate that Seth was not necessarily Adam's firstborn; the 'and he died' closure and total years are omitted in the slide; appears alongside Seth entry as a contrast pair. Covers Gen 5:3-4 only.

entries.translation:
And Adam [adam] was 130 years old
And he begat in his likeness, according to his image
And he called his name Seth
entries.match_notes: Three-line poetic rendering of Gen 5:3 alone used to debate whether 'his image' refers to Adam's image or God's image; 'adam' retained with brackets; repeated 3 times. Distinct from the Week 12a prose version above.

entries.translation: And Seth lived 105 years. And he begat Enosh. And Seth lived after he begat Enosh 807 years. And he begat sons and daughters.
entries.match_notes: Paired with the Adam entry (Gen 5:3-4) to illustrate that Enosh was not necessarily Seth's firstborn; the 'and all the days of Seth' closure and death notice are omitted. Covers Gen 5:6-7 only (no total years or death).

entries.translation: And Enoch lived 65 years. And he begat Methushalakh And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methushalakh 300 years. And he begat sons and daughters And all the days of Enoch, 365 years. And Enoch walked with God, And he was not, for God took him.
entries.match_notes: Enoch genealogy entry block; retains 'Methushalakh'; distinctive 'he was not, for God took him' phrasing

entries.translation: And Lamech lived 182 years. And he begat a son. And he called his name Noah, saying, 'This one, he will give us relief from our work, and from the toilsome labor of our hands from the ground which the Lord cursed.' And Lamech lived after he begat Noah 595 years. And he begat sons and daughters. And all the days of Lamech, 777 years. And he died.
entries.match_notes: Lamech entry block; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'relief' (not 'comfort') for Noah's name; 'toilsome labor of our hands'

entries.translation: When mankind began to multiply on the face of the ground and daughters were born to them, the sons of God saw the daughters of mankind, that they were good. And they took them wives from all that they chose. And the Lord said, 'My Spirit will not strive with mankind for ever, since he is flesh. The days of his life will be 120 years.' (Now the nephilim were in the land in those days â€” and even after â€” when the sons of God went into the daughters of men and they bore to them. They were the mighty men of old, renown men.)
entries.match_notes: Sons of God block; repeated 6 times; distinctive 'My Spirit will not strive' and 'renown men' phrasing; retains 'nephilim'

entries.translation: And the Lord saw that the bad of mankind was great in the earth and all the formation of his heart only bad all day long. And the Lord regretted that he had made mankind on the earth. And he was grieved in his heart. And the Lord said, 'I will wipe out mankind whom I have created from upon the face of the ground, from mankind to cattle, to creeping things, to birds of the heavens. For I regret that I have made them.' But Noah had found favor in the eyes of the Lord.
entries.match_notes: Pre-flood divine decision block; repeated 4 times; distinctive 'the bad of mankind' (not 'wickedness'), 'wipe out' (not 'destroy'), 'grieved in his heart'

entries.translation: And the Lord saw that the evil of mankind had become great on the earth and all the formation of the thoughts of his heart was evil continually. And the Lord regretted that he had made mankind on the earth, and his heart was grieved. And the Lord said, 'I will wipe mankind, whom I have created, from the face of the earth--from mankind to animals, to creeping things, and birds of the heavens--for I regret that I have made them.' But Noah found favor in the sight of the Lord. Noah was a righteous man. He was blameless among his contemporaries: Noah walked with God. Now Noah begat three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth. And the earth was corrupt in the sight of God, and the earth was filled with violence. And God observed the earth and, indeed, it was corrupted, for all living creatures on the earth were corrupt. So God said to Noah, . . .
entries.match_notes: Explicit label '(Gen 6:5-13)' on the slide; rendered as a single continuous prose block bridging the Yahweh/Elohim narrative sections including toledot of Noah in v.9; repeated 3 times in the structural analysis discussion. Note: the individual sub-sections (6:5-8, 6:9-10, 6:11-13) already exist as separate entries in extracted.json with different source PDFs; this entry captures the full contiguous block as presented here. The slide explicitly identifies this as a translation 'retaining some Hebrew word order.'

entries.translation: This is the toledot of Noah. Noah was a righteous man. He was blameless among his contemporaries: Noah walked with God. Now Noah begat three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
entries.match_notes: Toledot of Noah introduction block; retains 'toledot'; distinctive 'blameless among his contemporaries' phrasing; repeated in structural analysis

entries.translation: And the earth was corrupt before God, And the earth was full of violence. And God looked upon the earth, and behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted its way on the earth. And God said to Noah, 'The end of all flesh is come before me, for the earth is full of violence through them; and behold, I will destroy them with the earth.'
entries.match_notes: Earth corruption block; repeated 5 times including discussion of 'ruined' as better translation; distinctive 'all flesh had corrupted its way'

entries.translation:
Make to you an ark of wood of gopher
      reeds you will make the ark
and you will cover it inside and outside with covering
entries.match_notes: Identified explicitly as the 'HEBREW CONSTRUCTION' of Gen 6:14a-b (the core command); the dimensions and floor/window details of 6:15-16 are described as 'added as background' but not quoted verbatim as a translation block; this three-line block is flagged as 'the main statement'; the note explains 'reeds you will make the ark' (not 'rooms/nests'); 'cover' = kaphar (atonement).

entries.translation:
And Noah did according to all that God commanded.
                        Thus he did!
entries.match_notes: Rendered in bold/emphatic form under heading 'NARRATIVE COMMENT'; the emphatic 'Thus he did!' reflects the duplicated verb construction in Hebrew (asah / ken asah); discussed as showing God-Noah dynamic: 'God speaks, Noah obeys.' Structurally significant as an emphatic close to the ark-building section.

entries.translation:
the windows of heavens
   the fountains of the deep.
entries.match_notes: Listed as part of the 'Crescendo' section under Gen 7:11-16 with 'cosmic terminology: the windows of heavens / the fountains of the deep.' Only these two phrases are presented as translated text; the rest of vv.11-16 is summarized structurally. Confidence low because this is only a phrase-level fragment of the passage.

entries.translation:
the flood was on the earth,
the waters increased,
the waters prevailed and increased greatly,
the mountains were covered,
all flesh died,
the waters prevailed 150 days
entries.match_notes: Six-clause crescendo summary of Gen 7:17-24 presented as climax of the flood's destructive peak; the clauses track a crescendo of intensity matching the Hebrew narrative but are summarized/condensed rather than directly translated verse by verse. Confidence low as these are structural summary phrases rather than a full verbatim translation.

entries.translation: And God remembered Noah, and all the wild animals and all the domestic animals which were with him in the ark And God caused a wind to pass over the earth and the waters subsided.
entries.match_notes: God remembers Noah block; repeated twice with alternative 'And the Spirit of God passed over the earth' noted as valid reading; distinctive 'wild animals / domestic animals' distinction

entries.translation: And he sent out the dove from with him . . . And it returned to him to the ark. . . And he sent out his hand. And he took it. And he brought it to him to the ark. . . . [He sent out the raven . . .]
entries.match_notes: The bird-sending episode is cited in ellipsis form (suspension dots indicate portions not quoted) as a focused narrative illustration of the dove's return; the raven's account is given only as a single-line contrast 'He sent out the raven . . .'; the dove verses (8:8-12) are the focus. The mention of '40 days' context (v.6) and 'torn olive leaf' (v.11) confirms this covers 8:6-12 even though the full text is not displayed consecutively. Confidence medium because full text is fragmented/elliptical.

entries.translation:
You and your family exit the ark
   Cause the animals to exit so they will multiply
entries.match_notes: Speech-content summary in Elohim speeches table for Gen 8:16-17; two-clause summary under 'Elohim spoke to Noah saying.' Low confidence because this is a table paraphrase, not a displayed translation block.

entries.translation: Noah building an altar to Yahweh
entries.match_notes: Listed in the narrator's comments table as '8:20 - Noah building an altar to Yahweh' under the Yahweh column of a structural analysis table (contrasting with Elohim references); single-phrase rendering, not a full verse quote. Confidence low as this is a summary paraphrase in a table, not a block translation.

entries.translation: And the Lord said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground on account of man, for the thought of man's heart is evil from his youth; And I will not again destroy every living thing, as I have done. Henceforth, all the days of the earth, seed time and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night, will not cease.
entries.match_notes: Post-flood divine speech block; distinctive 'said in his heart' and full cycle list; explicit Gen 8:21-22 heading

entries.translation: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said to them, Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth. And let the fear of you and the dread of you be upon every animal of the earth, and upon all birds of the heavens upon all that moves on the ground and upon all the fish of the sea: Into your hand they are delivered. Every moving thing that lives will be food for you. As the grain I give you everything. Only, the flesh with its life, its blood, you shall not eat. And indeed, your blood, the blood of your lives, will I require: At the hand of every animal will I require it. At the hand of man, at the hand of each the blood of his brother, I will require the life of man. Whoever sheds man's blood, by man shall his blood be shed; for in the image of God he made man. But you all, be fruitful and multiply: swarm on the earth, and multiply on it.
entries.match_notes: Post-flood blessing block; distinctive 'As the grain I give you everything', 'swarm on the earth'; explicit Gen 9:1-7 heading

entries.translation:
I'm raising my covenant with you
   I will not again cut off all flesh
   I will not again destroy the earth by flood
entries.match_notes: Summarized in a speech-content table under 'Elohim said, to Noah and to his sons, saying' for Gen 9:9-11; rendered as three brief clauses paraphrasing the speech content (not a full verbatim translation). Medium confidence because this is a table summary format rather than a full verse display block.

entries.translation:
This is the covenant
   My bow will be a sign
   The waters of flood will not again destroy all flesh
entries.match_notes: Summarized in the same speech-content table under 'Elohim said' for Gen 9:12-16; three clauses covering the bow/sign portion; described as 'continuation of the same speech, but structurally separated for content purposes.' Medium confidence for same reason as 9:9-11.

entries.translation: The covenant between me and all flesh
entries.match_notes: Final line in the Elohim speeches table for the flood account: 'Elohim said, to Noah / The covenant between me and all flesh.' Single-clause summary closing the covenant section. Low confidence as this is a table entry, not a displayed translation block.

entries.translation: For this commandment that I command you this day is not too much for you, neither is it far off. It is not in the heavens, that you should say: "Who will go up for us to the heavens, and bring it to us, that we should hear it and do it?" And it is not beyond the sea, that you should say: "Who will go over the sea for us, and bring it to us, that we should hear it and do it?" For the word is very near to you, in your mouth and in your heart, that thou can do it.
entries.match_notes: Indented block under 'EXAMPLE' header in the Firmament/Heavens section; used to illustrate inaccessibility of the heavens and seas; cited as Deut 30:11-14

entries.translation: He reached forth from above, He took me out of great waters He delivered me from my strong enemy
entries.match_notes: Indented block cited as Ps 18:16-17, used to illustrate God restraining the waters of the deep as a threat to life; Day 3 lecture section

entries.translation: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the expanse demonstrates the work of his hands.
entries.match_notes: Indented block appearing three times under 'THE FIRMAMENT IN SCRIPTURE' section; cited as Ps 19:1

entries.translation: Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul. I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing; I am come into the depths of waters, and the flood overflows me. . . Deliver me out of the mire, let me not sink; let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the depths of waters.
entries.match_notes: Indented block cited as Ps 69:1-2, 14; used alongside Ps 18:16-17 to illustrate God restraining threatening waters; ellipsis in slide indicates abbreviated quotation

entries.translation: He knows our form, he remembers that we are dust
entries.match_notes: Inline block in the nakedness/1 John 1 cross-reference section; cited as Ps 103:14; used to show God is not ashamed of human nakedness/weakness

entries.translation: The LORD has established his throne in the heavens, and his kingdom rules over all.
entries.match_notes: Inline block under 'THE WATERS ABOVE IN SCRIPTURE' as dwelling place of God; cited as Ps 103:19

entries.translation: To you I lift up my eyes, O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
entries.match_notes: Inline block in the 'THE WATERS ABOVE IN SCRIPTURE' list; cited as Ps 123:1

entries.translation: [no block-formatted translation present in slides; cited as the only other biblical occurrence of Hebrew darash + 'life' in the same sense as Gen 9:5]
entries.match_notes: Reference-only citation, no quoted translation block. The lecturer uses Ps 142:4 to establish that 'pursuing life' (darash) means protecting/caring for life rather than chasing down a killer. No gold translation extractable.

entries.translation: Praise him, you heavens of heavens, and you waters that are above the heavens.
entries.match_notes: Opening line under 'THE WATERS ABOVE IN SCRIPTURE' block; cited as Ps 148:4

entries.translation: Hallelujah! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in the firmament of his power.
entries.match_notes: Indented block appearing three times under 'THE FIRMAMENT IN SCRIPTURE' paired with Ps 19:1; cited as Ps 150:1

entries.translation: Michtam of David. 1. Preserve me, Oh God, for I have taken refuge in you. 2. I said to the Lord, 'You are my Lord. My well-being is not beyond you.' 3. Regarding the holy ones which are in the earth, and to the majestic ones … all my delight is in them. 4. Their sorrows will be multiplied that hasten after other gods. 5. I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood, and I will not lift up their names upon my lips. 6. The Lord is the portion of my allotment, and my cup. You maintain my lot. 7. The lines have fallen unto me in pleasantness; indeed, a beautiful inheritance is mine. 8. I will bless the Lord who has counseled me; indeed, during the nights my heart corrects me. 9. I have set the Lord before me continually, because he is at my right hand, I will not be moved. 10. Therefore, my heart rejoices and my glory exults; indeed, my flesh dwells in security. 11. Because you will not forsake my soul to Sheol, you will not give your holy one to see corruption. 12. You will show me the path of life, and completeness of joy with your face, and pleasantness at your right hand forever.
entries.match_notes: User's preferred Psalm 16 translation. Note: user uses 12-verse numbering (splits standard v4 into v4-v5); user's v6-v12 = standard v5-v11. Handwritten annotations on the source image were intentionally excluded.

entries.translation: Woe to them who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness
entries.match_notes: Indented block at end of Day 3 summary section; cited as Isa 5:20

entries.translation: Over the heads of the living creatures there was the likeness of an expanse, shining like awe-inspiring crystal, spread out above their heads. And under the expanse their wings were stretched out straight, one toward another. And each creature had two wings covering its body . . . And there came a voice from above the expanse over their heads. . . And above the expanse over their heads there was the likeness of a throne, in appearance like sapphire; and seated above the likeness of a throne was a likeness with a human appearance. . . Such was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD. And when I saw it, I fell on my face, and I heard the voice of one speaking.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block under 'THE FIRMAMENT IN SCRIPTURE'; cited as Ezek 1:22-28, also Ezek 10:1; illustrates the firmament as God's throne-room expanse

entries.translation: Don't be careful about your life, what you should eat and what ye should drink; nor for your body what you should put on. . . . These things are what the nations seek after. Your heavenly Father knows that you have need of all these things. So seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
entries.match_notes: Indented block presented as a full slide after the 'to serve and keep the garden' section; cited as Matt 6:25-33; ellipsis indicates abbreviated quotation

entries.translation: Come to me, all you that are weary and heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me. My yoke is easy and my burden is light, and you will find rest for your souls.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block appearing as a standalone slide; PDF cites 'Matt 12:28-30' but the actual quoted text is Matt 11:28-30 (the 'Come to me...yoke is easy' passage). The Matt 12:28 reference in the slide is a PDF error.

entries.translation: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God. The Word was with God in the beginning. All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created. In him was life, and the life was the light of mankind. And the light shines on in the darkness, but the darkness has not mastered it.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block appearing five times in this PDF under 'GENESIS 1 AS METAPHYSICS - Biblical Confirmation'; cited as John 1:1-5, NET. Same passage also alluded to in Week 3b, Week 5a.

entries.translation: Truly, truly, I say to you, We speak that which we know, and we bear witness to what we have seen, and you don't receive our witness. If I have said earthly things to you, and you don't believe, how, if I say heavenly things to you, will you believe? And no one has gone up into heaven, except he who came down out of heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block under 'EXAMPLE - Jesus to Nicodemus' in the Heavens section; cited as John 3:11-13

entries.translation: Abide in me and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself unless it abide in the vine, thus neither can you unless you abide in me. I am the vine; you are the branches. He that abides in me and I in him, he bears much fruit; for without me you can do nothing. Unless anyone abide in me, he is cast out as the branch and is dried up; and they gather them and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block following John 1:1-5 in the 'Life is not thru the Logos but in the Logos' section; cited as John 15:4-6

entries.translation: Now this is eternal life â€” that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you sent.
entries.match_notes: Inline indented quotation in the 'what is eternal life?' sub-section; cited as John 17:3 NET. Also appears in Week 3b as 'That they might know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.'

entries.translation: Therefore, just as through one man sin entered the world and through sin, death, thus also death has come upon all men, since all have sinned.
entries.match_notes: Indented block under 'The Serpent - Animal or Satan?' section; paraphrased context given as 'Romans: sin entered the world through the sin of humanity'; cited as Romans 5:12 based on content match

entries.translation: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the surpassing greatness of the power may be of God, and not from us.
entries.match_notes: Indented block under 'The Essence of the Man' section, illustrating that humanity is formed from dust but animated by God; cited as 2 Cor 4:7

entries.translation: But all things having their true character exposed by the light are made manifest; for that which makes everything manifest is light.
entries.match_notes: Indented block appearing twice under 'WHAT IS LIGHT?' section; cited as Eph 5:13 DBY (Darby translation)

entries.translation: . . . so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
entries.match_notes: Inline block under 'ANCIENT COSMOS IN SCRIPTURE' alongside Exod 20:1; cited as Phil 2:10; illustrates the three-realm cosmology (heaven, earth, waters under the earth)

entries.translation: That which was from the beginning, that which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes; that which we contemplated, and our hands handled, concerning the word of life; (and the life has been manifested, and we have seen, and bear witness, and report to you the eternal life, which was with the Father, and has been manifested to us:) that which we have seen and heard we report to you, that you also may have fellowship with us; and our fellowship is indeed with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block, vv 1-3, in the 'Life is not thru the Logos but in the Logos' section alongside John 15:4-6; cited as 1 John 1:1-3. NOTE: verse numbering uses '2' for v1 header then re-uses '2' for v2 â€” PDF encoding quirk, content is vv 1-3.

entries.translation: 1 That which was from the beginning, that which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes; that which we contemplated, and our hands handled, concerning the word of life; 2 (and the life has been manifested, and we have seen, and bear witness, and report to you the eternal life, which was with the Father, and has been manifested to us:) 3 that which we have seen and heard we report to you, that you also may have fellowship with us; and our fellowship is indeed with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ. 4 And these things we write to you that your joy may be full. 5 And this is the message which we have heard from him, and declare to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all. 6 If we say that we have fellowship with him, and walk in darkness, we lie, and do not practice the truth. 7 But if we walk in the light as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanses us from all sin. 8 If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us. 9 If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins, and cleanse us from all unrighteousness. 10 If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block vv 1-10 under header 'LIGHT IN 1 JOHN 1'; expanded form of the same passage appearing as vv 1-3 in Week 2b; this is the most complete rendering. Used to parallel Gen 1 'light' theology.

entries.translation: . . . Jesus Christ. He is the only true God and eternal life.
entries.match_notes: Inline quotation in the 'LIFE IN JOHN' section, paralleling John 17:3; cited as 1 John 5:20; partial quotation (ellipsis at start indicates abbreviated text)

entries.translation: Because you say, "I am rich, and I have become rich and have need of nothing, but don't realize that you are wretched, and pitiable, and poor, and blind, and naked," I counsel you to buy from me gold purified by fire, that you can become rich, and white clothing, that the shame of your nakedness not be exposed, and eye salve to put on your eyes so you can see.
entries.match_notes: Full indented block appearing twice in the clothing/nakedness section paralleling Gen 3; cited as Rev 3:17-18

entries.translation: So the great dragon, the ancient serpent, the one called "Devil" and "the Satan," the one who deceives the whole world, was cast down to the earth.
entries.match_notes: Indented block under 'The Serpent - Animal or Satan?' parallel to the Eden serpent; PDF cites 'Rev 12:19' but that verse number is incorrect â€” the description of the dragon cast down is Rev 12:9. Likely a PDF typo.
